figures.caption: Figure 6: Sensitivity of our method to different Lagrangian multipliers. We vary the multiplier settings (ξ1, ξ2) and report the resulting agent-usage and routing-accuracy dynamics. Across these choices, the learned controller remains stable and performs well: the usage ratios stay well controlled, while the routing accuracy remains consistently high after convergence.

Table 1: Accuracy comparison for two heterogeneous multi-agent systems with different controllers and agent pools. We abbreviate MATH-lighteval as M-LEval, Omni-MATH as Omni, MinervaMath as Minerva, and AGI-Eval-MATH as AGI-M. Overall, our method achieves the highest average accuracy among baselines in both systems under usage constraints on Agents 1 and 2.

Table 3: Accuracy comparison on seven math datasets for two multi-agent systems using alternative controller models under usage constraints on Agents 1 and 2. We abbreviate MATH-lighteval as M-LEval, Omni-MATH as Omni, MinervaMath as Minerva, and AGI-Eval-MATH as AGI-M. The performance advantage of our method is preserved in both systems.

Table 8: Controller prompt templates for multi-turn coordination. Template I corresponds to the initial-turn prompt c, which performs routing based solely on the input query. Template II corresponds to the subsequent-turn prompt c′, which verifies the previous agent’s solution and either terminates the interaction or routes the problem to a stronger agent.
Table 9: Hyperparameter settings used by our method in the main experiments for Systems 1 and 2, corresponding to the results in Table 1 and Figure 3.

Figure 3: Training dynamics for two multi-agent systems with different controller and agent-pool configurations. System 1 uses Qwen2.5-7B-Base as the controller with agents Qwen3-30B-A3BInstruct (Agent 1), Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct (Agent 2), and Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct. System 2 uses Qwen3-4B-Base as the controller with agents Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct (Agent 1), Ministral-3-8BInstruct (Agent 2), and Llama-3-2-1B-Instruct. Compared with Router-R1, our method significantly improves reward and accuracy with slightly higher usage on the strongest agent.

Table 2: Comparison on seven math benchmarks for two heterogeneous multi-agent systems with and without Lagrangian multipliers. We abbreviate MATH-lighteval as M-LEval, OmniMATH as Omni, MinervaMath as Minerva, and AGI-Eval-MATH as AGI-M.
